title: Indulgent Slovakian Duck-Confit-Leg Confit
description: A rich and comforting Slovakian dish featuring slow-cooked duck confit leg, served with a side of kombu-cold-extract sauce and melted cheese.
instructions: Season the duck legs with salt and black pepper.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Sear the duck legs until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes per side.
Remove the duck legs from the pot and set aside.
Add the garlic to the pot and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the chicken stock, white wine, and water to the pot and bring to a boil.
Return the duck legs to the pot and cover with a lid.
Transfer the pot to the preheated oven and braise for 2 hours, or until the duck is tender and falls off the bone.
Remove the pot from the oven and let cool slightly.
Strain the braising liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean pot and discard the solids.
Bring the braising liquid to a simmer over medium heat.
Reduce the heat to low and let simmer for 10 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened slightly.
Stir in the kombu cold-extract and cook for 1 minute, until heated through.
Slice the duck legs into thick slices and serve with the kombu-cold-extract sauce and melted cheese.
Sprinkle with fresh parsley and serve immediately.
